campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: Campbell City Council met to accept a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help pay for youth recreation scholarships. The council also discussed plans to build a bike and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue, including reviewing feasibility studies and applying for grant funding to help pay for the project.
keyTopics: $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen for youth scholarships
Bike and pedestrian bridge planned over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue
Grant application being submitted to fund the Hamilton Avenue crossing project
Extension of interim ordinance number 2326 under consideration

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026, where residents could voice their opinions on a new rule or policy. After hearing from the public, the council moved forward with introducing the new ordinance and skipped the usual first reading step to speed up the process, planning to vote on final approval at the next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather community input
New ordinance introduced and fast-tracked for approval
Final vote scheduled for next regular council meeting
First reading waived to expedite the process

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 1, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to organize and finalize their upcoming meeting agendas for April 7th and April 14th, 2026. The council reviewed which new items should be added to each agenda and set specific times for certain discussions. They also reviewed public records from the previous week.
keyTopics: Finalized the April 7, 2026 meeting agenda
Organized the April 14, 2026 draft agenda
Set specific time slots for agenda items to keep meetings on schedule
Reviewed public records from March 19-26, 2026

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a routine meeting on March 30, 2026 where they approved previous meeting minutes and opened the floor for public comments and questions. The council also reviewed reports from various committees and discussed upcoming meetings for city boards and commissions.
keyTopics: Approval of previous meeting minutes
Public comment period for residents
Committee and staff reports
Schedules for upcoming board and commission meetings
Recreation Division contact for agenda questions (650-903-6331)

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Housing and Human Services Commission met to review applications for federal funding that helps pay for affordable housing and community programs. They also approved notes from their previous meeting and heard presentations from groups seeking money to support local housing and services.
keyTopics: Federal housing grant funding applications being reviewed
Affordable housing and community development projects
Approval of February meeting notes
Presentations from organizations seeking funding

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The city council focused on helping local businesses grow and succeed. They discussed offering tax breaks to businesses that haven't paid their license fees, reaching out to shopping centers, and running campaigns to encourage residents to shop locally. The council also explored ways to partner with private companies to sponsor summer events and attract new retail stores and startups to Cupertino.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program - helping past-due businesses catch up without penalties
Shopping Center Outreach - directly engaging with local malls and shopping areas
Summer of Celebrations Shop Local Campaign - encouraging residents to buy from local businesses
Public-Private Partnerships for Events - bringing in sponsorships to fund city events
Economic Incentives for Startups and Retail - tax breaks and support to attract new businesses to Cupertino

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 2, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara's Historical and Landmarks Commission held a hearing on April 2, 2026 to review proposals affecting the city's historic buildings and sites. Residents could attend in person or join online via Zoom to watch and participate in the discussion.
keyTopics: Historical and Landmarks Commission hearing for historic property reviews
Hybrid meeting format allowing both in-person and remote participation via Zoom
Public comment submissions accepted by email until noon on meeting day
Remote participation options: Zoom link or phone dial-in available

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — March 17, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a routine meeting on March 17, 2026, to handle administrative business like approving previous meeting minutes and scheduling future meetings. The council approved Mayor Montano's travel to a 2026 wellness conference and confirmed a new representative to serve on the school district's economic committee.
keyTopics: Approved meeting minutes and confirmed the city council's calendar of upcoming meetings
Mayor Montano authorized to attend WELL 2026 Annual conference
New Milpitas Unified School District representative appointed to Economic Committee
Routine administrative approvals for city operations

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026 to recognize Women's History Month and approve two main items: an agreement with the American Red Cross to use city facilities as disaster shelters, and a three-year contract with Insight Public Sector to provide email security and management services for about $93,400 per year.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
Red Cross disaster shelter facility agreement
Email security software contract ($93,383/year for 3 years)
Late agenda items added to meeting
